title: Netanyahu continues war against Hezbollah in Lebanon despite Iran ceasefire
subhead: The Israeli prime minister stressed that Donald Trump's truce does not apply to Lebanon, contradicting his Pakistani counterpart, who is mediating the conflict and said the suspension of hostilities is meant to apply 'everywhere.'

title: French hostages return home after more than three years of 'daily horror' in Iranian prison
subhead: Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris landed in Paris on Wednesday morning, following years of detention on espionage charges.

title: Arthur Kenigsberg: 'Orban is the only European leader simultaneously backed by Putin and Trump'
subhead: Arthur Kenigsberg, the president of the think tank Euro Créative, highlights the allegiances of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who finds himself in a difficult position ahead of the looming legislative elections on April 12.

title: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
subhead: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

title: 'The US administration's dysfunctions and the Iranian regime's resilience are driving Trump further into an obsession with force'
subhead: Five weeks into the US bombing campaign against Iran, Donald Trump's trademark unpredictability has been overtaken by the unpredictable dynamics of the war itself, observes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
subhead: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.

title: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
subhead: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

title: 'He said if the kids pulled off the job, there'd be many more': The strange foiled attack on Bank of America in Paris
subhead: Three teenagers and a 21-year-old man were placed in detention as part of the investigation into the attempted bombing of Bank of America's office in Paris. Investigators suspect that Iranian intelligence services were behind the plot.
